free_text: Measurement of average cross sections and isomer
ratios for natRe(g,xn) reactions at the end-point
bremsstrahlung energies of 30 MeV and 40 MeV.
The linear electron accelerator LUE-75
at the AANL, Yerevan, Armenia.
Bremsstrahlung with end-point energies 30 and
40 MeV.
Natiral rhenium target (purity - 99.99%).
ORTEC HPGe detector.
The flux-weighted average cross sections of
natRe(g,xn) were obtained using bremsstrahlung
gamma-rays.
Compiled at the Russia MSU SINP CDFE by
V.Varlamov.
The flux-weighted average reaction cross sections.
Statistical uncertainties.
Natural copper consists of two 63Cu (69.17%) and 65Cu
(30.83%) isotopes. The reaction 65Cu(g,n)64Cu was
used as a monitor reaction in reallity because the
64Cu isotope is produced only from the 65Cu isotope
by (g,n) reaction. The photon flux was determined by
using the photo-peak area based on the activity of
511 keV 35.2% g-line of 64Cu.
Data from Table 3 of
Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B,507,7,2021.
The flux-weighted average reaction cross sections.
Statistical uncertainties.

Experimental values for isomeric ratios were obtaines
using measured data on average SIG-m and SIG-tot
cross sections.
Statistical uncertainties.

Data from Table 4 of
Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B,507,7,2021.
Semi-empirical isomeric ratios, TALYS 1.9.
Semi-empirical isomeric ratios, EMPIRE 3.2.
No uncertainties given.
The direct measurement of ICR (isomeric cross section
ratio) for 186m,gRe isomeric pairs is impossible due
to the long half-life of the 186mRe isomeric state
(2.0 x 10**5 y). The half-life of the 186gRe ground
state is short (3.72 d), therefore the experimental
average cross section measurement is possible.
In order to evaluate the ICR for 186m,gRe isomeric
semi-empirical method was used which consits of the
combination of theoretical and experimental data. In
order to obtain the theoretically calculated cross
section for the formation of 186Re, the TALYS 1.9 and
EMPIRE 3.2 nuclear codes were applied.
The theoretically calculated cross section for the
formation of 186Re obtained using the TALYS 1.9
nuclear code.
The theoretically calculated cross section for the
formation of 186Re obtained using the EMPIRE 3.2
nuclear code.
Data from Table 5 of
Nucl.Instrum.Meth.B,507,7,2021.
